text.tspan: Both solve: 'I need data from multiple services in one query'
but they trade off LATENCY vs FRESHNESS vs OPERATIONAL COMPLEXITY
OFTEN USED TOGETHER:
u2022 simple aggregation u2192 API composition
u2022 search & dashboards u2192 CQRS read model
u2022 the BFF picks the right strategy per query
On each query, FAN OUT to source services and aggregate.
PROS:
u2022 Always FRESH (real-time)
u2022 No replicated data; no consistency issues
u2022 Easy to start with: just call services and merge
u2022 Each service's API stays the source of truth
CONS:
u2022 Latency = slowest service in the fan-out
u2022 Partial failure handling required
u2022 N+1 risk with nested aggregation
u2022 Hard to do complex JOINs / GROUP BY across services
BEST FOR:
u2022 Simple aggregation (composing existing data shapes)
u2022 Low-latency-tolerant clients
u2022 Real-time freshness required
Pre-build a denormalized view fed by EVENTS from source services.
Query hits ONE store; data is already joined and indexed.
PROS:
u2022 Very FAST reads (one DB lookup)
u2022 Supports complex queries (full-text, aggregates, sorting)
u2022 No fan-out latency, no partial failure
u2022 Can use specialized store (Elasticsearch, columnar)
CONS:
u2022 Eventually consistent (lag = event propagation time)
u2022 Requires event streams (Kafka, etc.)
u2022 Read model needs schema design, maintenance, rebuilding capability
u2022 More infrastructure to operate
BEST FOR:
u2022 Complex queries (search, dashboards, reports)
u2022 High read volume
u2022 Eventual consistency acceptable
u2022 Use-case-specific projections
